- The Attachment Theory: How Childhood Affects Life: Kind: captions Language: en The attachment theory argues that a strong emotional and physical bond to one primary caregiver in our first years of life, is critical to our development. If our bonding is strong and we are securely attached, then we feel safe to explore the world. We know there is always that safe base, to which we can return to anytime. If our bond is weak, we feel insecurely attached. We are afraid to leave or explore a rather scary-looking world. Because we are not sure if we can return. People who are securely attached are said to have greater trust, can connect to others and as a result are more successful in life. Insecurely attached people tend to mistrust others, lack social skills and have problems forming relationships. There is one type of secure attachment and there are 3 types of insecure attachments: Anxious/Ambivalent Anxious/Avoidant and Anxious/Disorganized. In responses to distress, the first 3 react organized, while the last acts disorganized. To understand the theory better, let's look at Mr and Ms Smith, who have 4 children. Luka, Ann, Joe and Amy. The Smiths are lovely parents, who cuddle, make frequent eye contact, speak warmly, and are always there for their kids. But one day Mr Smith falls very sick and dies. For Mrs Smith life now becomes very difficult. She spend all day working, while at the same time trying to care for her children. And impossible task. At 6 years of age, Luka’s brain is for the most part developed, his character strong and his world view shaped. The new situation does not affect him much - he knows there still is always mom - his safe-haven. He feels securely attached. Later he turns into a trusting and optimistic young man. His self image is positive. Ann, who is 3, has problems coping with the new lack of attention. To Ann, her mother now acts unpredictably. She is anxious about their relationship, and as a result becomes clingy. To get her mom’s attention, she has to raise her emotional state and scream. When her mom finally reacts with a predictable response, she herself acts ambivalent and doesn't show her true feelings. Later in life, others think Ann is unpredictable or moody. Her self image is less positive. Her attachment style Anxious Ambivalent. 2-year old Joe, spends his days with his uncle, who loves him, but thinks that a good education means being strict. If little Joe shows too much emotions or is too loud, his Uncle gets angry and sometimes punitive. This scares Joe. He learns that to avoid fear, he has to avoid showing his feelings - also in other situations. As an adult he continues this strategy and has problems to enter relationships. His image of himself is rather negative. His attachment is: Anxious Avoidant Amy, who is just one year old, gets sent to a nursery. The staff there is poorly trained, overworked and often very stressed. Some are outright abusive. Amy therefore becomes anxious of the very people she seeks security from - a conflict which totally disorganizes her ideas about love and safety. As she is experiencing fear without resolution, she tries to avoid all social situations. As an adult she thinks of herself as unworthy of love. Her self image is very negative. He attachment is anxious disorganized Our attachment is formed in the very first years of our lives, a time when we are too young to communicate our anxiety and as a result can experience high levels of stress. Then our adrenal gland - an organ sitting on top of our kidneys - produces the stress-hormones adrenaline and cortisol. The heart rate increases, the blood pressure goes up and we become alert. If that happens frequently, it is called toxic stress. Toxic, because it impairs the development of a child's brain, and weakens the immune system. In embryos or at a very young age, toxic stress can even switch the expressions of genes, which can affect our health many decades later. By simulating a Strange Situation, we can assess an attachment style, already by the age of one. To do this, we let the child play with their mothers for a few minutes inside a room. Then the child is left alone. The key moment is the child's reaction when her mother returns. Securely attached children first usually hug their mother, then can calm down and eventually get back to playing. Insecurely attached children can be ambivalent and avoidant. Some can't stop crying or refuse to continue playing. The long term effects of our attachment in the early years, are well documented. Using the theory, researchers at Minnesota University were able to predict already at age 3, if a child would dropout of high school with 77% accuracy. In another study, undergraduates at Harvard were asked to assess how close they felt to their parents. 35 years later they were ask about their health. 91% of those who said they had a rather broken relationship with their mother, were also diagnosed with health issues, including coronary artery disease, hypertension, and alcoholism. For those that had reported a warm relationship, the figure of poor health diagnosis was just 45%. But there is another reason why the early years deserve special attention. They are the starting place for subsequent behaviors. A kid that feels securely attached at age 2, can make friends at kindergarten. Their worldview gets reinforced with every interaction and they develop optimism. As a result they make good relationships at school, then at colleague and later at work. Highly insecurely attached children can miss out on this opportunity. Psychologist John Bowlby, a pioneer in attachment theory, allegedly said, “What cannot be communicated to the mother, cannot be communicated to the self.” In other words: those who feel insecurely attached, might not quite understand themselves. To get to know who they are and what they feel, they might have to go way back in time.

  19. @시낭-e2g: 1. 서사적 구체성 vs 논리적 보편성

유튜브 애니메이션은 이론을 '이야기'로 치환합니다. Smith 씨네 가족이라는 구체적인 대상을 설정하여, 부모의 죽음이나 방치 같은 개별적 사건이 아이의 성격에 미치는 과정을 보여줍니다. 이는 소비자에게 '지식'이 아닌 '경험'으로 다가가며, 시청자는 캐릭터의 눈물과 표정을 통해 이론을 즉각적으로 체감합니다.

반면, **텍스트 매체(학술서)**는 모든 개별적 특수성을 제거하고 보편적인 개념을 정립하는 데 집중합니다. '슬퍼하는 아이'라는 이미지 대신 '영아기의 분리 불안'이라는 용어를 사용하여, 감정적 동요 없이 현상을 객관적으로 분석하게 합니다. 유튜브가 **"내 이야기처럼 느끼게 하는 것"**이 목적이라면, 텍스트는 **"개인적 감정을 배제하고 현상의 법칙을 발견하는 것"**에 목적을 둡니다.

2. 동시적 정보 흡수 vs 선형적 사유의 축적

유튜브 매체의 가장 큰 특징은 시각과 청각의 동시성입니다. 나레이션이 흐르는 동안 그림이 그려지는 방식은 소비자가 정보를 처리하는 시간을 매체가 강제로 통제하게 만듭니다. 이러한 '감각적 동기화' 기법은 짧은 시간에 많은 정보를 주입하는 데 탁월하며, 소비자는 별도의 해석 과정 없이 매체가 보여주는 흐름에 몸을 맡기게 됩니다.

반면, 텍스트 매체는 독자의 시선이 활자를 따라가는 선형적(Linear) 흐름을 따릅니다. 독자는 읽다가 멈춰 서서 앞 내용을 다시 확인하거나, 자신의 과거를 떠올리며 깊은 사유에 잠길 수 있습니다. 즉, 유튜브가 정보의 **'파지력(기억에 남는 힘)'**을 높이는 매체라면, 텍스트는 정보 사이의 여백을 통해 **'통찰(깊은 깨달음)'**을 이끌어내는 매체라는 본질적 차이가 발생합니다.

3. 알고리즘의 유혹 vs 맥락적 권위

유튜브라는 매체 환경에서 애착 이론은 **'알고리즘의 선택을 받기 위한 상품'**으로 구성됩니다. 제목에 "인생이 망가지는 이유"와 같은 자극적인 후킹(Hooking)을 배치하고, 7분 내외의 짧은 호흡으로 정보를 압축하는 것은 매체의 **'유통 권력'**이 알고리즘에 있기 때문입니다.

이와 대조적으로 텍스트 매체는 방대한 각주와 참고 문헌, 엄격한 문어체라는 **'형식의 권위'**를 통해 유통됩니다. 유튜브가 "지금 당장 클릭해야 할 흥미로운 정보"로 자신을 포장한다면, 텍스트는 "시간을 들여 탐구해야 할 엄밀한 진리"라는 무게감을 전달합니다. 이는 소비자가 정보를 대하는 태도(가벼운 소비 vs 진지한 탐구)를 결정짓는 매체적 특성으로 이어집니다.

🟧 심화된 이해: 매체는 지식을 어떻게 재창조하는가?

결론적으로 두 매체의 차이는 단순히 '글이냐 그림이냐'의 문제가 아닙니다.

유튜브는 무거운 심리학 이론을 **'안전한 우화'**로 재구성하여 대중의 심리적 방어기제를 무너뜨리고 즉각적인 자아 성찰을 유도하는 **'정서적 촉매제'**로서 작용합니다.

텍스트는 파편화된 정보를 **'구조적 체계'**로 엮어내어 현상의 근본 원인을 추적하게 만드는 **'이성적 설계도'**의 역할을 수행합니다.

동일한 내용이라 할지라도 유튜브에서는 **"공감과 치유"**의 언어로, 텍스트에서는 **"분석과 증명"**의 언어로 재탄생하는 것입니다. 이러한 매체 간 비교를 통해 우리는 현대 사회에서 지식이 소비되는 방식이 얼마나 매체 의존적인지를 명확히 파악할 수 있습니다.

- The EASIEST Way to Develop Color Film at Home: Kind: captions Language: en hi i'm Linus and welcome back to the CineStill&nbsp; studio if you've ever been intimidated by the idea&nbsp;&nbsp; of processing color negative film at home don't&nbsp; worry cinestill has simplified the process with&nbsp;&nbsp; a two-bath solution that allows you to develop up&nbsp; to 24 rolls in this video we're going to show you&nbsp;&nbsp; how to develop beautiful color negatives in&nbsp; just a few minutes using the CineStill Cs41&nbsp;&nbsp; color simplified two-bath process i've personally&nbsp; developed hundreds of roles using this method in&nbsp;&nbsp; the past couple years so here's what you'll&nbsp; need a developing tank two one quart pitchers&nbsp;&nbsp; two one quart chemical storage bottles a CineStill&nbsp; Cs41 kit either liquid or powder and optionally&nbsp;&nbsp; a TCS-1000 Temperature Control System. The&nbsp; CineStill Cs41 Color Simplified Two Bath Process&nbsp;&nbsp; is made up of two chemical solutions developer&nbsp; and blix plus an optional final rinse additive&nbsp;&nbsp; inside the box you'll find a printed set&nbsp; of instructions and seven small bottles of&nbsp;&nbsp; chemical concentrate which are clearly labeled&nbsp; if you have the powder version of the kit you'll&nbsp;&nbsp; find three bags of powdered concentrate we'll&nbsp; be using the TCS-1000 Temperature Control System&nbsp;&nbsp; to mix and heat our chemistry but you can simply&nbsp; use a clean stir stick if you don't have a tcs yet&nbsp;&nbsp; now let's start with the developer first grab one&nbsp; of your 1 quart mixing pitchers and pour 20 ounces&nbsp;&nbsp; of clean water into it next while stirring&nbsp; or circulating with the TCS-1000 pour in the&nbsp;&nbsp; contents of the bottle labeled developer part A&nbsp; and mix well then do the same for developer part B&nbsp;&nbsp; and C the total volume of the solution should be&nbsp; one quart so top it off with some water and then&nbsp;&nbsp; continue mixing once fully mixed you should rinse&nbsp; your TCS-1000 to clean out any chemical residue&nbsp;&nbsp; from inside the unit place the TCS-1000 into a&nbsp; clean pitcher of water and circulate replace the&nbsp;&nbsp; water and circulate again to rinse pour the&nbsp; mix developer solution into one of your one&nbsp;&nbsp; quart chemical storage bottles and secure the cap&nbsp; tightly make sure to label both the bottle and the&nbsp;&nbsp; cap to prevent any accidental cross-contamination&nbsp; now we'll mix the blix pour 18 ounces of clean&nbsp;&nbsp; water into a clean pitcher next while stirring or&nbsp; circulating with the tcs pour in the contents of&nbsp;&nbsp; the bottle labeled blix part a and mix well&nbsp; then do the same for blix parts b and c the&nbsp;&nbsp; exact details of mixing the blix solution with the&nbsp; powder version of the kit are slightly different&nbsp;&nbsp; so be sure to read the instructions carefully the&nbsp; total volume of the solution should be one quart&nbsp;&nbsp; so top it off with some water and then continue&nbsp; mixing once fully mixed you'll again want to&nbsp;&nbsp; rinse out your TCS-1000 to clean out any blix residue from inside the unit place the TCS-1000&nbsp;&nbsp; into a clean pitcher of water and circulate&nbsp; replace the water and circulate again to rinse&nbsp;&nbsp; pour the mixed blix solution into your other one&nbsp; quart chemical storage bottle and label it clearly&nbsp;&nbsp; a note on chemical contamination here while&nbsp; the two bath process is designed such that&nbsp;&nbsp; the developer can get into the blix without&nbsp; deactivating the blix it only takes a few&nbsp;&nbsp; drops of blix getting into the developer to kill&nbsp; it aside from rinsing the equipment you're using&nbsp;&nbsp; while mixing it's critical that the chemistry&nbsp; is always poured back into the correct bottle&nbsp;&nbsp; and the correct cap is attached labeling both&nbsp; the bottles and the caps appropriately is one&nbsp;&nbsp; of the simplest and easiest ways to avoid making&nbsp; a painful mistake now loading your developing tank&nbsp;&nbsp; before developing you'll need to load your film&nbsp; into a developing tank we've got a whole video&nbsp;&nbsp; showing you how to load several different kinds of&nbsp; developing tanks over here we at cinestill are big&nbsp;&nbsp; fans of the JOBO 1500 series and the 1520 tank&nbsp; is one of my go-to's during a single processing&nbsp;&nbsp; session it can hold either two rolls of 35mm or two rolls of 120. if you don't have&nbsp;&nbsp; access to a darkroom you can load your film in a&nbsp; changing bag we've also made a video on how to use&nbsp;&nbsp; a film changing bag that you can find here now the&nbsp; moment you all have been waiting for processing&nbsp;&nbsp; your film after your film is loaded into your&nbsp; developing tank and your chemistry is ready to go&nbsp;&nbsp; you'll be able to start processing your color&nbsp; negative film of the two chemicals the developer&nbsp;&nbsp; is the more temperature sensitive one and designed&nbsp; to be processed at 102 degrees fahrenheit there&nbsp;&nbsp; are multiple ways of achieving and maintaining&nbsp; this temperature you can use the TCS in a plastic&nbsp;&nbsp; tub as a water bath or simply place the TCS&nbsp; directly into your pictures of chemistry which is&nbsp;&nbsp; the way we'll be processing today if you process&nbsp; outside of a temperature-controlled water bath be&nbsp;&nbsp; sure to compensate for the temperature drop during&nbsp; processing by increasing the starting temperature&nbsp;&nbsp; by a degree or two okay let's get processing&nbsp; once your developer is up to temperature&nbsp;&nbsp; quickly and carefully pour enough developer&nbsp; into your tank to completely submerge the reels&nbsp;&nbsp; inside and fasten the lid this minimum volume is&nbsp; usually printed somewhere on the developing tank&nbsp;&nbsp; fresh developer will require 3 minutes and 30&nbsp; seconds at a temperature of 102 degrees fahrenheit&nbsp;&nbsp; with continuous agitation for the first 10 seconds&nbsp; and then at least four inversion cycles every 30&nbsp;&nbsp; seconds as you continue to use developer to&nbsp; process your film you'll have to increase&nbsp;&nbsp; developing time to compensate for the partially&nbsp; weakened solution more details on chemical reuse&nbsp;&nbsp; and adjusting developing times can be found in&nbsp; the instruction manual linked in the description&nbsp;&nbsp; inversion style agitation looks like this invert&nbsp; the tank while also spinning it on its axis&nbsp;&nbsp; in a smooth and steady motion between inversion&nbsp; cycles it's also good practice to tap the bottom&nbsp;&nbsp; of the developing tank to dislodge any air&nbsp; bubbles that could cause uneven development&nbsp;&nbsp; at the end of the developing time remove&nbsp; the lid from the tank and pour the developer&nbsp;&nbsp; out combining it with any excess chemistry&nbsp; still in the pitcher or storage bottle quickly and carefully pour in the required&nbsp; volume of blix and fasten the lid the blix&nbsp;&nbsp; will require 8 minutes at a temperature between 75&nbsp; and 105 degrees Fahrenheit with the same agitation&nbsp;&nbsp; schedule as the developer continuous agitation&nbsp; for the first 10 seconds and then at least&nbsp;&nbsp; four inversion cycles every 30 seconds unlike with&nbsp; the developer you won't need to increase the blix&nbsp;&nbsp; time as you continue to use it at the end of the&nbsp; blix time remove the lid from the tank and pour&nbsp;&nbsp; the blix out recombining it with any excess&nbsp; chemistry still in the pitcher or the storage&nbsp;&nbsp; bottle congrats your film is now processed and&nbsp; it just needs to be washed thoroughly place the&nbsp;&nbsp; developing tank under running water at about the&nbsp; same temperature as your blix and wash for a&nbsp;&nbsp; minimum of three minutes alternatively you can&nbsp; fill and empty your tank with water seven times&nbsp;&nbsp; modern color films have stabilizers in the&nbsp; emulsion released through the two bath process&nbsp;&nbsp; what's commonly referred to as the stabilizer step&nbsp; these days is simply a final rinse containing a&nbsp;&nbsp; mild anti-microbial and surfactant and can&nbsp; help prevent mold from growing on your film&nbsp;&nbsp; if you live in a particularly humid area for&nbsp; most people if you store your film in archival&nbsp;&nbsp; negative sleeves in a cool dry place using&nbsp; this chemical solution is completely optional once your film is fully washed you can hang it up&nbsp; to dry refer to this video if you're having issues&nbsp;&nbsp; with water spots on your film due to hard water&nbsp; it's good practice to weigh down the bottom of&nbsp;&nbsp; the film strip with a weighted clip or something&nbsp; similar to prevent film from curling as it dries&nbsp;&nbsp; drying time is dependent on relative humidity and&nbsp; temperature the chemistry you use to process your&nbsp;&nbsp; film should be recombined with any excess&nbsp; chemistry in each respective storage bottle&nbsp;&nbsp; the developer and blix baths are intended to be&nbsp; reused and can be stored for several weeks if&nbsp;&nbsp; you're unsure whether or not your chemistry is&nbsp; still usable you can conduct a snip test we've&nbsp;&nbsp; got a video on snip tests right here exposure to&nbsp; air uv light and heat will speed up the aging of&nbsp;&nbsp; your chemistry always store your chemicals in&nbsp; dedicated chemical storage bottles with as much&nbsp;&nbsp; air squeezed out of them as possible this couldn't&nbsp; be easier with our accordion style bottles which&nbsp;&nbsp; collapse down to evacuate excess air now keep&nbsp; your bottles in a cool dark place away from&nbsp;&nbsp; the reach of children previewing your negatives&nbsp; once your film is dry it's ready to be inspected&nbsp;&nbsp; well-developed color negative films should have&nbsp; dense clearly visible edge printing and a fully&nbsp;&nbsp; cleared film base that is translucent not cloudy&nbsp; if your film roll comes out blank you guessed it&nbsp;&nbsp; we've got a video covering that too you can use a&nbsp; bright white light source to preview your inverted&nbsp;&nbsp; images using our cinestill cs41 color negative&nbsp; inversion filters on Instagram there are a number&nbsp;&nbsp; of film presets along with simple controls that&nbsp; allow you to adjust brightness and color balance&nbsp;&nbsp; hit the link in our description to check out&nbsp; our instagram filters and make sure you save&nbsp;&nbsp; them to your instagram camera so you don't&nbsp; [Anne] The hippocampus, this area towards the middle of the brain, that differed according to parenting styles. The infants who had received less responsive caregiving, their hippocampi were a bit bigger. [Anne] This was surprising, because the hippocampus is really important to learning and to managing distress. So what it suggests is that the babies were having to manage their own stress, because they're not getting quite as much support from their parents. Even at six months, these everyday differences in parenting were actually linked to observable differences in the structure of the infant brain. [Anqi] Hey! Good job. To my knowledge, that was the first time a group had seen this. [Anne] When a parent is attentive and responsive, the baby is learning that the world is a safe place, and that frees up time for them to explore their environment. When a baby doesn't get those same signals from the parent... ...the baby may need to prioritize thinking about safety and comfort rather than exploration. I'm the last person to say that parenting is easy. None of us do this perfectly. What is important is that the overall experience that the child is receiving is one of attentive and responsive care. And I think, for parents, that's reassuring. - The One Simple Budgeting Method That Changed My Life: Kind: captions Language: es Para ordenar tus finanzas de manera efectiva, necesitas un método de presupuesto que sea realmente simple. Llevo casi una década aplicándolo con éxito: administrarte a ti mismo como si fueras un negocio. Hoy veremos qué significa esto en la práctica y cómo puedes hacerlo con el método exacto que les mostraré a continuación. Lo recomiendo al cien. Si te administras como un negocio rentable, al final de cada mes tendrás ganancias restantes, o sea, utilidad neta, para seguir operando tu negocio por mucho tiempo. Así, asegurarás su continuidad y crecimiento a largo plazo. Los negocios rentables se mantienen a flote porque constantemente ganan más dinero del que gastan. Entonces, el paso uno para administrar tus finanzas como un negocio es al menos ser consciente de tus finanzas personales. Según un estudio de psicología de la Universidad de Rice, ser consciente de tus finanzas es una de las razones más grandes por las que la gente mantiene y de hecho mejora sus finanzas personales. ¡Qué sorpresa! Ya sé, pero escuchen este estudio. El profesor realizó tres estudios sobre la relación entre el conocimiento personal de activos, pasivos y patrones de gasto, concluyendo: "Un mayor grado de autoconciencia sobre las propias finanzas se traduce en retroalimentación sobre éxitos y fracasos pasados con el dinero, menor incertidumbre sobre las fortalezas y debilidades financieras actuales, y claridad sobre cómo avanzar". Para no hacerles el cuento largo, esto no debería sorprenderlos. Mientras más atención le pones a algo, mejor te vuelves y menos inseguro. Claro, a menos que seas yo buscando novia, donde más atención no equivale a más éxito. Por eso prefiero las finanzas: más atención, generalmente mejora. Ya en un tono más serio, usualmente tengo amigos que vienen y me preguntan: "Oye, ¿cómo puedo mejorar en la administración de mis finanzas personales?". Y siempre les pongo el mismo reto. Que es registrar sus ingresos y gastos por al menos tres meses, casi al centavo. Los que realmente tienen éxito en mejorar sus finanzas son los que logran cumplir este reto y que también revisan su cuenta de banco, sus estados de cuenta de la tarjeta de crédito y sus cuentas en línea con cierta frecuencia. Los que revisan sus cuentas al menos una vez a la semana o más, suelen ser mejores con sus finanzas personales. Y tiene sentido, porque sabes exactamente cómo vas. Yo, por ejemplo, reviso mi banco diario o cada dos días, y eso me da mucha tranquilidad sobre cómo voy financieramente. De hecho, si han visto algunos de mis otros videos, sabrán que tengo mi famosa hoja de control financiero donde sumo todos mis ingresos y gastos al final de cada mes. Así que, esa es una gran razón por la que el último día del mes es tan divertido para mí. Por ejemplo, hoy es treinta de octubre y mañana será treinta y uno de octubre, que es Halloween, sí, pero también es fin de mes, cuando me toca sumar mis ingresos y gastos. Me gusta tomar una Coca-Cola de Dieta al final de la noche. Reviso mis cuentas, registro todo y veo cómo fue el último mes. Luego, reviso mi progreso anual: ¿crece mi patrimonio neto?, ¿mis inversiones?, ¿mis ahorros?, ¿mis deudas? Así, tengo una visión completa. Definitivamente es uno de mis días favoritos. Y... literalmente salivo solo de pensar en... lo mucho que me divierto a fin de mes. Ahora,... a algunas personas no les gustan tanto sus finanzas como a mí. Algunos de mis amigos a los que les pongo ese reto, el reto de registrar los gastos por tres meses, etc., les da bastante pena si gastan veinte dólares en su café de Peet's o cien dólares en una camiseta de diseñador de vez en cuando. Y simplemente... en general... les da miedo enfrentar sus finanzas. Y ese sentimiento de vergüenza en torno a tus finanzas es un sentimiento muy común, y de hecho podría abrumarte al punto de que no seas capaz de registrar tus gastos por lo culpable que te sientes al respecto. Y si estás viendo este video y haces exactamente eso, solo tienes que saber que eso está... completamente arraigado en la psicología, y hay algunas formas de superarlo. Para muchos, enfrentar su realidad financiera es algo que les asusta muchísimo, porque confrontarla puede ser verdaderamente aterrador. Quizás tengas veinte mil dólares en deudas, préstamos estudiantiles, un saldo alto en tu tarjeta de crédito o incluso un saldo negativo en tu cuenta bancaria. Sabes que al revisar tus estados de cuenta, te confrontará directamente con la cruda verdad de tu situación actual. Sin embargo, creo que lo mejor es confrontar tu deuda y la realidad actual. Necesitas empezar a registrar tu progreso desde ahora. Puede que ahora debas veinticinco mil dólares, y eso, la verdad, no se sentirá nada bien. Pero si empiezas a registrarlo, te garantizo que, al ver tu progreso en los próximos meses, te motivará muchísimo a seguir adelante con todo. He registrado mis finanzas anualmente por seis o siete años. Incluso al principio, cuando apenas ganaba treinta mil o cuarenta mil dólares al año, supe que, a medida que progresaba, las cosas iban por buen camino. Es muy gratificante ver seis años atrás cómo estaba y darme cuenta de cuánto he avanzado. Este registro se convierte en una bitácora financiera, una herramienta muy poderosa para ganar confianza en la vida y en tus decisiones económicas. Si quieres mejorar tus finanzas, yo haría cuatro cosas. Uno: empieza a registrar tus gastos. Será manual. Revisa tus estados de cuenta bancarios y de tarjeta de los últimos tres meses para promediar gastos por categoría y tener una visión clara. Dos: revisa los saldos de tu banco y tarjeta de crédito en línea, al menos una vez a la semana, o más. Número tres, empezaría un presupuesto, y vamos a usar el método de presupuesto exacto que yo uso en un momento. Y número cuatro, aprendería a desvincular tus gastos de tu identidad. Al principio, este último punto puede ser bastante complicado de aplicar, sobre todo si eres muy consciente y sientes vergüenza por tus gastos actuales. si te acostumbras a registrar tus gastos e ingresos, con el tiempo te transformarás en un observador externo. Empezarás a ver cómo gastas tu dinero, pero casi como si fueras un tercero, alguien ajeno a la situación. logras ser completamente objetivo con tus finanzas. Es un sentimiento difícil de articular, pero cuando gasto dinero, casi no siento que sea yo quien lo hace. Me veo a mí mismo desde la distancia, como un observador externo, casi como un alienígena viendo a Humphrey y sus gastos. Lo veo de forma objetiva, así que no me siento mal al gastar. Solo pienso: "Gasté veinte dólares en un café de Peet's". Ojalá llegues a un punto en el que sientas poca o ninguna emoción por tus gastos. podrás ver tus gastos objetivamente de forma regular. Una vez consciente de tus finanzas, podemos ver el método de presupuesto que cambió mi vida, al que llamo P&L de mi vida, o estado de pérdidas y ganancias. Un P&L es un estado financiero que usa un negocio para ver su rentabilidad mensual, detallando ingresos y gastos. Así se ve uno de muestra. Arriba, este negocio tuvo tres mil trescientos sesenta y siete dólares en ventas mensuales. Abajo, gastaron mil cuatrocientos dólares en costo de ventas: materiales, importaciones, subcontratistas, inventario y flete. Eso dejó una utilidad bruta de mil novecientos sesenta y siete dólares con veinte centavos. Aún tenían gastos como sueldos de Tom y Mary, teléfono y papelería, sumando mil ciento ochenta y cinco dólares, dejando una utilidad neta de setecientos ochenta y dos dólares con veinte centavos al final del mes. Visualmente, este estado de cuenta funciona de arriba hacia abajo. Arriba ves los ingresos generados, y conforme bajas, cuánto gastaron para mantener el negocio, o sea, su costo de ventas. Por último, cualquier otro gasto variable estaba hasta abajo. Tener esta ganancia de setecientos centavos al final del mes, en el caso de este negocio, les permite tomar esta utilidad y ahora tienen mucha flexibilidad sobre qué hacer. Podrían reinvertir en el negocio, o tal vez sacar ese dinero y comprar un PlayStation 4. La idea es tener una ganancia restante cada mes para el futuro. ¿Cómo se relaciona esto con nosotros? Este es el método exacto que cambió mi vida. Veamos la hoja de estado de resultados y simplifiquémosla en tres o cuatro categorías. Para nuestro estado de resultados personal, mantendremos los ingresos arriba. Probablemente recibes un sueldo, así que sabes tu ingreso mensual. Para el costo de ventas, vamos a reemplazarlo con la categoría llamada gastos fijos. Estos son los gastos necesarios para tu vida. Por ejemplo, cosas como la renta, el seguro, el transporte, los gastos médicos, etcétera. La sección de gastos la reemplazaremos con gastos discrecionales. Son tus gastos opcionales mensuales, aquellos que puedes controlar y ajustar. Como salir a comer, ir de compras, entretenimiento y videojuegos. Y por último, la utilidad neta, esa la vamos a dejar exactamente igual. Si esto te parece confuso, no te preocupes, tendré una plantilla exacta que puedes descargar en la descripción. Básicamente, recreé este estado de resultados, pero con celdas vacías para que lo llenes con tu información. este es el método de presupuesto exacto que vamos a usar. Vamos a crear estados de resultados para cada uno de los meses que existamos. Y con suerte, tendremos algo de utilidad neta al final de cada mes. Este método de presupuesto es de hecho muy similar al llamado método de presupuesto base cero, que es bastante popular en la comunidad de finanzas personales. Ahora, en ese método, lo que tienes es tu ingreso mensual arriba, y luego enlistas todos tus gastos. E idealmente, al final de cada mes, te quedan cero dólares, para que cada dólar tenga un destino. en el método del estado de resultados, la diferencia es que te va a sobrar dinero al final de cada mes, y con ese dinero sobrante, puedes decidir qué quieres hacer. Puedes ahorrarlo para compras grandes, para tu retiro, o simplemente mantenerlos como dólares flexibles para el próximo mes. La mejor parte de hacer estos estados de resultados, en mi opinión, es que después de dos, tres o cuatro meses de datos, vas a descubrir cuánto estás gastando en promedio en cada categoría. Y de esa manera puedes ver una categoría, tal vez como entretenimiento o compras, y decir: "Ok, ¿estoy gastando demasiado dinero en esta categoría?" y como que cuestionarte y ver si puedes reducir o recortar en ciertas categorías. Idealmente, en el estado de resultados de tu vida personal, tienes utilidad neta al final de cada mes. Eso nos dará flexibilidad para asignar ese dinero estratégicamente a diferentes metas financieras. Así que el siguiente paso es cómo asignar la utilidad neta mensual. Ahora, si recuerdan, hice un video en el canal llamado "Los siete lugares a los que tu dinero debería ir cuando te pagan". Ese va a ser un video súper a fondo sobre exactamente cómo deberías asignar tu dinero cada vez que te pagan. Haré un resumen abreviado para este video. Queremos que al final de cada mes nos sobre dinero (utilidad neta) para fortalecer nuestra base financiera. Cubierta la base, buscaremos incrementar nuestro patrimonio, invirtiendo y haciendo crecer nuestros activos. en cuanto a nuestra base financiera, veámoslo de esta manera. En la base de todo, quieres al menos asegurarte de hacer los pagos mínimos de tus deudas, como tus tarjetas de crédito, tus préstamos estudiantiles y los de tu auto. La razón es que si no tienes cubierto este primer nivel de la base, que es hacer los pagos mínimos de tu deuda, podría derrumbar toda la estructura porque tu puntaje de crédito podría arruinarse por completo. Solo omitir un pago en cierto tipo de deuda podría desplomar tu puntaje hasta ciento ochenta puntos. Lo cual es una locura porque eso va a afectar tus futuras oportunidades de... tal vez financiar un coche o una casa o financiar lo que sea que quieras en tu vida. Así que, cuando se trata de deudas, tenemos que ser perfectos al hacer los pagos de esa deuda. Pero suponiendo que ya tenemos eso cubierto, el siguiente nivel es crear un fondo de emergencia. Deberías tener al menos seis meses de gastos de vida en un fondo de emergencia. Es decir, si gastas dos mil al mes, tendrías que tener doce mil ahorrados. Ponlo en una cuenta de ahorros de alto rendimiento, porque doce mil dólares al 4 o 4.5% con las tasas actuales, te dan al menos quinientos dólares extra al año. Si buscan recursos para una cuenta de alto rendimiento, les dejaré algunos enlaces abajo. Muy bien, tras cubrir esos dos niveles fundamentales, hay dos caminos que puedes tomar, y son bastante intercambiables. El primero es liquidar todas tus deudas, excepto la hipoteca. Esto te permitirá vivir un estilo de vida mayormente libre de deudas, dándote mucha paz mental y flexibilidad. La segunda es que empieces a aportar a tus cuentas de retiro, como Roth IRA, IRA o 401(k). De estas dos opciones, creo que la número uno, liquidar tu deuda, fortalecerá tu base financiera, mientras que la número dos, aportar a tus fondos de retiro e invertirlos, buscará incrementar tu patrimonio con el tiempo. Aquí es donde se pone un poco complicado en términos de asignar tu dinero. Realmente no sé cuál es la correcta para ti, específicamente porque la situación de cada persona es un poco diferente. Personalmente, yo liquidaría toda mi deuda, incluyendo préstamos estudiantiles y tarjetas de crédito. La única deuda que podría excluir es la hipoteca, pero depende de tu tasa de interés. Si llegaste hasta aquí, con tu base financiera cubierta y quizás aportando al retiro, el siguiente paso es invertir. En una cuenta de inversión, busca optimizar el interés compuesto para aumentar tu patrimonio neto de manera significativa a largo plazo. Puedes adquirir activos como bienes raíces o inversiones alternativas, pero invierte en lo que entiendas, no solo por lo que digan tus amigos. Sea lo que sea, sigue tus inversiones y su rendimiento; eso te hará un mejor inversionista a largo plazo. Mucha gente ni siquiera llegará a adquirir activos porque siguen atorados en la base financiera por su mala administración. Pero si te administras como un negocio, te garantizo que verás progreso en tu vida financiera. Podrás enfrentar tu realidad financiera. Y eso es lo que importa: mientras progreses hacia tus metas, eso ya es una victoria. Si este video te aportó valor, suscríbete y checa mis plantillas gratuitas. El método de presupuesto de hoy está enlazado abajo, gratis. Si te interesa asignar tu dinero, checa mi siguiente video aquí... sobre "Siete lugares a los que tu dinero debería ir al cobrar". Nos vemos en el próximo video. Nos vemos.

  3. @BeanCounter86: Great vid. 

A couple things: 
1) Car Insurance 
2) Car maintenance savings 
3) Life Insurance (assuming health insurance is taken out before take home pay)
4) Emergency savings (Pay it like a bill. Don't make it an afterthought.)
5) Retirement savings  (Pay it like a bill. The earlier you start, the more compound interest works in your favor).

Recommend going through your last three months of savings to see what your bills really are instead of relying on memory. Don’t forget annual bills. Break them down into 12 monthly payments and drop that into a savings account so that you’ll have the cash saved when it’s time to pay.

Also, if you know you want to take a vacation, buy a car, buy a house, make any kind of large purchase, think about how much you want to save for that, divide it by the number of months you have until you go on the trip or make the purchase and pay THAT like a bill as well. 

Example: You plan to buy a car with a $300 car payment. At least 12 months before you buy the car, start saving $300 + estimate on car insurance + monthly maintenance into a savings account. Use that as down payment on the car. When you pay the car off, transfer that same amount to the "car savings account" and work toward buying your next one for cash.

Happy budgeting!

us on average our flips are taking about five months because those things always happen you may get the ones that go perfectly and then you may get the nightmares and then you may get the ones in between but for us it averages to about five so make sure you're giving yourself a realistic timeline the second big mistake i see is very similar to this and that is underestimating the rehab cost so i'll see a lot of people look at a house and say oh man i could get this done for twenty thousand dollars when in reality it's a forty thousand dollar job and maybe they just don't know how to run the numbers and they don't know what it truly will cost or maybe in their mind they're thinking that they can do everything themselves and get it done for twenty thousand dollars whatever the case may be you should not run your numbers that way get a true rehab cost and a true rehab cost is when you go out to hire someone else to do this job because you will never scale your flipping business if you are the one doing the work on your home you personally can only work on one home at a time you cannot do 10. so start estimating your rehab costs the right way the third big mistake i see is also part of the rehab process and that is actually overdoing the rehab and i see a lot of flippers get into that mindset of well i wouldn't want this in my house so i need to do this it's like no flipping houses is a business you have to make money to be profitable and to continue doing it later on you're not building your dream home every time you flip a house some examples of over renovation that i see some flippers will take the popcorn ceiling out of every single house and we'll take it out of a bunch of houses as well but if all the comps have popcorn sealing and that's what the buyers expect why am i going to get rid of the popcorn ceiling it may not add any value to the home yet it costs me more money so anytime you're renovating you want to make sure you're getting at least two to one for your renovation dollars so what i mean by that is if you spend one dollar in renovation it should yield two dollars in profit and if removing popcorn ceilings cost you a thousand dollars but it makes you no extra money or even a thousand dollars more then you shouldn't do it it would have to make you two thousand dollars for it to be worthwhile i see the same thing with countertops flooring and other finishes at the end of the day you have to renovate based on the comps i have a saying i tell my students copy the comps the comps have already proved what works they've told you what the value is what kind of renovation you need to do to hit that value there's not really anything confusing about it just copy them so don't over renovate the fourth mistake i see a lot of flippers make is speculating on the arv for us anytime we flip we want to just use what's already sold so if i see a comp at 300 000 and it's in very similar condition to what i'm going to make my home then i am anticipating that i'm going to sell this house for 300 000. and it works both ways i don't want to be too conservative if i say it's 280 or 290 i'm going to lose the deal because somebody's going to pay more than me but i don't want to say oh it's probably going to get 310 or 320. there's not many other houses on the market mine's going to be better you don't want to speculate on other things you can't control like i said before for us i want to copy the comps if the comp is 300 000 and it's done at this level of rehab that's exactly what i want to do i don't want to overdo my rehab because if i do then i'm trying to get that 310 320 and the appraiser may not give me the value for it so i would rather just try and copy what's already been done and i don't want to speculate and try and get an arv that doesn't exist yet the fifth mistake i see is people not factoring in money cost so the money cost or whatever it costs you to get the loan for that property now if you're buying at all cash okay you don't really have money cost but many people starting out don't realize how expensive money can be if you've got to pay a couple of points and you're paying a high interest hard money loan that is going to play a big role in your profit i see a lot of new flippers saying well my purchase price is this i'm going to minus out my rehab costs i'm going to minus out my realtor commissions i'm minusing out closing cost and there's my profit like no you're still missing money cost which is a huge part of the equation you're also missing your holding costs like utilities taxes insurance but the money costs are what will burn a lot of people so before you make all these offers on all these flips make sure you understand what your money cost is and money cost is very similar to rehab cost if you've got money and you're going all cash that's great but you're gonna run out of cash at some point we all only have so much money so start evaluating deals as if you did it's just like the rehab yeah maybe you can do the rehab yourself for this one but you can't do that on all the other flips in order to scale your business you have to start hiring people out using other people's money and having these costs on every single deal that's how we run our business i'm not trying to cut cost on a particular section to make a deal work i'm not saying i've never done that i've done it plenty of times and that's why i'm telling you not to do it any time i've ever tried to manipulate the numbers to make the deal work i'm usually going down a rabbit hole into a bad deal sometimes i'll say well maybe the arv could be this maybe we can get rehab down to this maybe we can get one of our lenders to do it for less any time you start asking yourself those questions that means the deal's probably not very good because if the deal was good you wouldn't be asking yourself those questions you'd be saying wow this is a great deal even despite all these costs we're gonna make a lot of money so that's a good bonus tip when you start questioning your costs trying to manipulate them to make them work you probably don't have a good deal so in conclusion let's recap the top five mistakes so you do not make them number one underestimating your timeline number two underestimating your rehab number three over renovating number four speculating on your arv and number So if I invested in my in my uncle's pizza shop, he's not keeping the money. He's paying it back out to me. So the aristocrats are companies usually over $1 billion, and they've been increasing their dividends, which means they pay you more and more every year for at least 25 years. And all of a sudden you're like, boy, I bought that really cheap. It's bought itself and paid for itself over and over again. I have this cash flow coming in. Has it been trending up? Can I make multiple streams of income on that one stock? Remember I said there's four I want that. Has the dividend been consistent for at least ten years? Has it been growing? I personally don't like looking at companies when I'm starting, and when I'm working with people that are building up their their initial little nest egg, I want at least ten years of increasing their dividend and paying it out. Is the PE ratio in a reasonable range, say 15 to 25, or are you buying something that's way overblown? A lot of people buy duplex or triplex, maybe even a small multifamily that's got four, six units. You live in one unit and you let the other units help cover the mortgage. Usually you're levering yourself into this. This is the house you're buying, and now you're learning landlords and you kind of have the training wheels on. It's the live in improvement strategy, which is you buy a primary residence that's maybe a little long in the tooth, maybe a little beat up, and you fix it up and you live in it for at least two years, and then you sell it. So for most people here is what you do (This is the FOO from the Money guys who have a great system that will get most people on track financially):

1.  Save enough money to cover your highest insurance deductible in cash
2.  Get all of your employer 401k/403b etc match (Hard to beat 100% rate of return)
3.  Pay off all high interest debt (credit cards, many consumer loans, etc)
4.  Save for an Emergency fund so you have enough money in cash for 3-6 months of expenses
5.  Max out your Roth IRA and, if applicable, HSA
6.  Max out retirement accounts (If you hit 25% of your gross income in your savings rate including your employer match move on to step 7)
7.  Save 25% of your gross income

You can look at steps 8&9 yourself.  If you start in your 20's you probably don't need to hit a 25% savings rate, but unfortunately most people start at age 35 which is why they chose 25% as the savings rate goal.

- How to be Organized at Work: 8 Tips to Increase Productivity: Kind: captions Language: en Being organized comes naturally to some, but to others, it’s a task in itself to others. That said, organization in the workplace is the key to productivity. So, if you want to learn how to be more organized at work – stick around! To support our channel, hit the subscribe button and get notified every time we upload new content. 1. Use To-Do Lists To-do lists help you stay on track and productive throughout the day. Use a to-do list to write down important things you want to accomplish, and organize your list based on priority. For example, list the top three tasks that you must get done at the top of the list and things that can wait until tomorrow at the bottom of the list. You can take your organization a step further by color coordinating tasks in order of urgency. 2. Use a Planner Journaling is a great way to keep on top of your progress throughout the days, weeks and years. Besides achievements, you can also keep note of upcoming meetings and set goals, so it’s always at the forefront of your mind. Depending on your preference, you can purchase a written or digital journal with dates or without. 3. Manage Your Time Managing your time effectively is a key skill to have in the workplace. You should know how long it takes you to complete a certain task, which allows you to then trim off unnecessary hours off your day. By allocating a specific time to tasks, you can also achieve more during your working day. 4. Learn to Delegate If you have a mountain of work to get through, it’s impossible for you to do it all on your own. So, if a project has a tight deadline, learn to delegate tasks to colleagues who are best equipped with handling it. If you can match the work to people’s skills, you will be able to get the highest quality of work completed in a reasonable amount of time. 5. Be an Early Bird As the age-old saying goes, “The early bird catches the worm first”, and if you look at all successful entrepreneurs, they all follow a similar daily routine that involves waking up early, allocating time for a workout or self-care before starting work for the day. So, if you want to be more organized, follow suit and create a great morning routine that makes you motivated to work hard. 6. Limit Distractions It’s easier to stay organized and productive when you limit distractions. By doing so, it allows you to stay focused on one task instead of jumping back and forth. Keep common distractions like your phone out of sight, and even consider muting Slack or Email notifications while you’re working towards a deadline. 7. Keep a Tidy Environment Keeping a clean and tidy working area makes your work more efficient. When you have a clean working environment, you don’t get distracted by the mess around you and don’t waste time looking for something that you need. So, if you ease towards the messy side, it’s time to do a bit of spring cleaning and tidy your workspace. 8. Take Regular Breaks Regular breaks are needed to stay focused and productive. So, give yourself a period of time (say 40 minutes) to crack down on some work before you take a short 5–10-minute break to recharge your batteries and refocus. If you have a sedentary job, use this time to get up and walk around or do some stretching to get your blood flowing. Now that you’re well-equipped with the best ways to be organized, you’ll be much more prepared in a working environment. But if you’re looking for more tips and tricks, visit CareerAddict.com for more advice. CareerAddict offers professionally designed resume templates for all career levels. Our ‘7in1 Resume Bundle’ includes a free job application checklist, which is specifically designed to help you secure an interview and bag a job offer. Get the bundle and transform your career. You can find the link in the description below. And don’t forget to ‘like’ the video and click on the ‘Subscribe’ button to get more insightful tips!

- Spring season raises concerns over trash buildup: Kind: captions Language: en issue they say is only getting worse now that it's springtime. >> As temperatures start to heat up, neighbors tell me trash is becoming more noticeable in their neighborhoods, and they say it's not just about what it looks like, but how it impacts their day to day lives. As temperatures climb, so does concern over how long trash is left sitting out in the heat. Waste breaks down faster, creating stronger odors and drawing in pests like rats and insects. In some neighborhoods, many say it doesn't take long for trash to pile up, especially between pickup days. >> I mean, you look at the area right now, it looks pretty solid, but there's definitely some stuff on the ground. >> For many, the issue goes beyond smell. They say it impacts how they use their own space, and whether they even want to spend time outside during the summer months. >> Clean up the area a little bit. You know what I mean? Like, let's spend a little more money. Resources here of quality. >> Life and state data shows the problem is much bigger than what's visible on one block. New York generates more than 42 million tons of waste each year, with the average person throwing away more than 4 pounds a day. Studies show a large portion of that trash, up to 80%, could be reused or recycled, but much of it still ends up in landfills. As summer approaches, some neighbors say they want to see changes, including more frequent trash pickups, additional public garbage cans, and stronger enforcement to prevent illegal dumping. >> When it comes to picking up litter garbage, people need to do a better job at looking out for like cans on the side of the road or garbage bags or anything like that just needs to be picked up and tossed in the trash, the correct trash receptacle. So. >> And with hotter days ahead, neighbors say keeping trash under control now could make a big difference in how clean and comfortable their neighborhoods

- "LONG DISTANCE, a Reel Story Short Documentary Film" (2025): Kind: captions Language: en [Music] So far away from for So far away from me. far away from The So far away from me so far away from Sh [Applause]

- Once poor, always poor? | DW Documentary: Kind: captions Language: en Berlin: Marzahn-Hellersdorf a district considered socially and economically disadvantaged. Many residents here are living beneath the poverty line. Including Pierre Hollberg and his family. "I budget about ten euros a day for groceries." That's for everyone two adults and four children sharing four rooms that make up just under 90 square meters. Grandma Evi lives on the same block, and helps out where she can. "Everything's getting more expensive, but we don’t have more money. There’s actually less.” Never before have so many people in Germany been living in poverty. Is it even possible to escape this fate? Six in the morning. Before Eveline Weyer wakes her grandchildren, she takes in the quiet. Her husband who has cancer is still asleep. Grandma Evi spent many years unemployed. Soon, she will retire. Two months ago, she learned that she also has cancer. Her family is struggling financially. "Yesterday I picked up a bit of fruit to make juice, so my husband can get his vitamins. That cost me 35 euros for a little bit of fruit and vegetables. It's madness. When I think that a cucumber my daughter's kids love cucumbers one cucumber already costs 1.19 euros. It's enough to make you sick. We won't even be able to afford that soon." Raised in former East Germany, Evi worked in agriculture, then for an ice cream factory. After reunification, however, she became unemployed. Every morning at seven, Evi helps care for her daughter’s family. Mandy lives with a severe disability. Six people live here, in very cramped quarters. "Wake up!" There's barely enough space for a wheelchair but accessible housing is hard to find. "You're already up again?" Phil, the youngest, is already in the living room where his parents are still sleeping. "Pierre, seven o'clock, Pierre." Grandma Evi prepares the school snacks. "Lea, one sandwich. Celine, one sandwich.... Joshua... Phil. In the evenings I ask what they want on their sandwiches, and then prepare everything in the morning. Celine is making her own now." Celine has to share her room with her younger brother, Joshua. "Celine, seven o'clock, come on, get up. You still need to make breakfast. Joshua, you need to get up too." "Okaaay." The kitchen, too, gets crowded. There's no room for a dining table. "Fresh cucumber, all good?" "Okay. Bye, Grandma." "Bye!" "Bye bye!" Once the fourth grandchild has left the house, Grandma Evi's attention turns to her daughter, Mandy. "She likes that, too." Mandy is a mother of six. Two of her children have already moved out. Since a serious stroke ten years ago, she's had an inoperable blood clot on her brain stem. She can barely speak, and needs special care. Evi and her son-in-law, Pierre, take turns looking after her. "That's when it was still good." Before Mandy's illness, Pierre worked as a salesperson. Ten years ago, he stopped working entirely, and has been a stay-at-home dad ever since. He often thinks about how things used to be. "That was a lovely time, of course. Mandy was still healthy. We shared the work things were going fine. Then, right in the middle of it, my wife had the stroke. After that, everything changed. Completely. That's just how it is. When I look at the pictures, my mind drifts back how it was, how it could have been... but how it isn't." Poverty can stem from many causes one of those is illness. Marzahn-Hellersdorf is home to nearly 300,000 people. In 2023, the percentage of people threatened by poverty was 18.1 percent well above the national average of 16.6 percent. Close to 14 percent of all teenagers in Hellersdorf leave school without a diploma. The charitable organization 'Die Arche,’ or The Ark, wants to change that. Pascal Höhn known to all as Kalle was once an 'Arche kid' himself. He knows firsthand the problems faced by disadvantaged youth. Now he’s 24, and training to become a social welfare assistant, and has a part-time job here. "Come on, over here." "No, I've just..." "Arms out. Come on." "Out of the way!" "Basically, I just try to be there to be someone who gives them a sense of security, you know? I remember how it was back then with nobody there. I missed out on a lot really a lot. I don't want them to go through the same thing. That's why I keep an extra eye on them." Kalle's mother was a single parent. His father disappeared early. In addition to poverty, he is still impacted by his father’s absence. He first came to Die Arche when he was nine. At first, things looked promising. "At 14, I stopped coming and that was my undoing. That's when the drugs really began. I was constantly just getting into fights, even lost a few teeth myself. Then things got so bad that I seriously hurt someone so badly he could barely move his arm, and could never work again. It should never have come to that. I want to keep the kids from having to experience that ruining their whole lives over one stupid moment, just to look cool. I served two years and seven months in prison for that offense and a few other things. That's why I feel a special responsibility to give other kids that security, to be there for them." Once a year, Die Arche hosts a big courtyard festival. Poverty affects all sorts of people especially children. "There's that kind of poverty where you have no food, no money, nothing. But there's also the kind where you just feel completely alone, where there's just no one there for you." "Hi. All good?" "Yeah, you?" "Yeah, course." "See you!" "Hey, Shady it's great you're wearing your glasses. I'm really proud of you. That's awesome." Josefine Brendel is a family support worker at Die Arche. She previously worked in a home for kids who had been removed from their families. At Die Arche, she supports children and their parents to ensure it never comes to that. Everyone just calls her Josi. "What I love most is when the kids are happy to see me. Each of them comes with their own issues. That's why I'm here to meet them right there, when they need help the most." "Reading and writing are key for educational success. Every week, Josi practices reading with Oskar. A therapy dog helps out as a quiet listener." "Pla...stic buck..et." "Say the whole word again?" "Plastic bucket." "Very good." "There's often laughing when someone mispronounces a word, or can't read properly. Here, though, they can overcome that fear because they're allowed to just read. We correct words together, and they read them again. And the dogs help too just petting them helps ease the children's fears." Single mothers are at an especially high risk for poverty. For a long time, Jessica Laue relied upon welfare. Even though she's working now, she barely makes more money than she did before. Jessica has been living with her children in a housing block for 14 years. Four rooms, 85 square meters, 700 euros with utilities. While her eldest son still lived at home, Jessica slept in the living room. Now only Lea and Alex live with her. "Lea, get two liters out. So... for 500 milliliters we need 50 grams of semolina. We're making four times that now." "Four. So now you want to know how much…" "...I want to know how much semolina we need." "Have you packed your folder for tomorrow? Your backpack I mean." "I still need glue sticks." "Okay." "Done!" "Anything else you want help with right now?" "Later." "Later, okay." Despite her two vocational qualifications, Jessica couldn't find work for years. "For my first dream job, I trained as a painter and varnisher. I actually began that while I was pregnant with my second child. I gave birth to Alex while I was doing it, and still completed my training." A back condition meant she was unable to work in that field, and she retrained as an industrial clerk. "Retraining with a toddler isn't easy but I managed it. I saw it through, and I passed. After that I kept looking for work, but as a single mom, I wasn't flexible which made job hunting harder. I didn't have a chance.” Instead, she worked as a volunteer. "It was important to me to do something and show my kids that you have to work." Seventeen-year-old Alex is doing well in school. As a passionate volleyball player, he's already won a few trophies. Now, he’s completing his vocational diploma, and applying to the military. He wants to become a soldier in the mountain infantry. "I plan to be really active there. I'll start with voluntary military service. I’ll become a soldier for four years and maybe extend or switch career tracks later." Alex sees that many of his peers are unable to escape the circumstances they were born into. "I think it's because a lot of kids see that their parents don't work, but still get money. So they ask why they should work. I don't see it that way I was raised differently. It's important to contribute your part to society so everything keeps running. You should earn your money honestly, and maybe secure better living conditions than you would have just sitting at home collecting benefits." As her meticulous budget shows: Even with a full-time job, Jessica doesn't earn enough to support her family. She needs government assistance. "Right now, I work and take home 1,418 euros net despite having the reduced tax rate for single parents. If I only had that income, we'd actually be worse off than on welfare, because I earn less than I used to receive. Back then I got nearly 1,500 euros in benefits." At the start of each month, she sets aside 150 euros for each week. "Every Monday, I take the money out and put it in the shopping fund that's for food, or outings with the kids. That's just for the weekly budget. I have to say, when I had the time and we were able to go to the food bank every week, it was much easier. That's why I say there's not really much more left financially now, compared to when I was just receiving welfare and volunteering. The extra money I earn now goes to things I didn't have to spend it on before." Wednesday is Evi's food bank day. Every other week she's allowed to collect donated groceries especially fruit and vegetables, which have become particularly expensive, even in low-cost stores. "I need cabbage..." "Yes." "Yes. Today, Grandma Evi is bringing home five bags of groceries. In a supermarket, all that would have easily cost over 100 euros. "I'll use all of it. For example, the cabbage I take that downstairs in the morning. Phil likes cabbage, so I always give him some. Parsley I've got my chopper, I chop it all up. I've got lots I can store and freeze. "So, how was school?" "Good." The only private space Joshua has is his loft bed. "You don't have a place to do your homework and stuff?" "Sure, up here. I even built myself a little desk." Joshua rarely has the quiet he needs to do his homework. His father Pierre barely has time to help the kids with schoolwork. He serves them lunch one after another. As a stay-at-home dad, he has no income. The family lives on Mandy's disability pension, housing assistance, child benefits, and child supplements. This family is sticking together in the face of poverty. Nationwide, nearly 18 million people in Germany are living in poverty. Escaping the cycle takes between four and six generations and no small amount of luck. Kalle is a frequent point of contact for 14-year-old Danny, who is being bullied at school. - becoming social is easy, actually: Kind: captions Language: en Okay. Don't tell anyone. But this video is sponsored by Incogni. So in case you didn't know how to make friends is actually the most Googled topic on my laptop. Google will just tell you to reconnect with your old friends wikiHow is just a copy paste Reskin of Google with questionable illustrations. Reddit comments will tell you to go on meetup websites that no one actually uses in real life. Or to start a cornhole club. Social media also probably won't help you. YouTube is the main reason people don't go outside in the first place. Tik Tok is literally designed to entertain you when you're alone. LinkedIn is a dystopian simulation. Facebook only has people twice your age. Snapchat messages are from people you don't know. Discord messages are from people you don't want to know. Instagram is where you send messages that no one replies to and Tinder is where you schedule dates that no one shows up to. Which leaves only one real option for making friends, and that's talking to people in real life. Luckily, after years of failing miserably at this, I figured it out and I came up with 16 different methods that you can pick from based on your personality and what comes naturally to you. Method 1: Filter out the boring people. It can often be frustrating to meet a group of people and take weeks or months to realize that your personality really isn't compatible with any of them. and the solution is to let out your natural, controversial, unfiltered self from the beginning so that you can quickly filter out people who aren't compatible with you. You shouldn't force it, but if a slightly funny dialog option pops into your head, or if you have a weird opinion, sometimes you should just say it instead of hiding it. And if some people react positively, congratulations, You've just identified your new potential friends Method 2: the unique interest. If you've ever been the filler friend who people don't seem to find that interesting or exciting, It's literally just because they don't know enough about you. So to fix this, Talk to people about your unique interests. You might not think people care, but even if you're into something niche like pressure washing driveways, At least people will have something to remember you by. When they first meet you, Just make sure that you explain your interest to people in a way they can understand without using big words and without getting into the complex stuff. If you can sense that the other person is bored, it means it's probably time to ask them about one of their interests. Method 3: Be outside of your head. Have you ever been in a group setting where everyone's talking and you don't know when to interject or even what to say? Man, I've been quiet for a while. What are people thinking about me right now? What do I even see? Do I have aura right now? quick. What tips can I use from that-&nbsp; that one video with Jordan&nbsp; Peterson in the thumbnail? this is overthinking. Also known as getting in your head. Overthinking is what happens when you get a thought. Then you think about it. Then filter it. Then imagine yourself saying it. Then modify it and approve it. And then send it out your mouth just to mumble it because the conversation's already moved on. But being outside of your head just means taking in the conversation. Getting thoughts, but not thinking them over too much. Just say them. And even if you say something cringe, see it not as a failure, but as practice. Method 4: Don't force it. when you feel like you need to force friendships with people who don't even like you. You feel pressure, and pressure leads to anxiety, which leads to not being your natural self. So if someone doesn't really care about you or you have absolutely nothing in common with them and you're not compatible, it's not the end of the world. Just be as nice as you can and move on. and at this point, it's a numbers game. So as long as you talk to new people, you will make new friends eventually. Method 5: be the main character. the people who have the most friends tend to be the people who initiate conversations the most. So just initiate conversations with any sentence you could possibly think of. Just assuming that everyone wants to talk to you. But remember, once you initiate, don't just talk about yourself. Ask simple questions about people's lives. Listen. And then ask follow up questions that show you actually listened to them. this part is crucial because eventually, once people get comfortable talking to you, they'll start initiating conversations with you. Method 6: be a side character. The book How to Win Friends and Influence People is one of the only books on social skills that actually works. I feel like books on social skills always try and teach you how to scientifically hack people's brains into producing oxytocin molecules by maintaining a 3-to-1 eye-contact- to-look-away ratio, or by strategically touching this part of the shoulder. But in this book, the main piece of advice is that people tend to be mostly interested in themselves. If you want someone to like you, make them feel important, compliment them, say their name when&nbsp; you initiate a conversation, act&nbsp; happy when they walk in the room, remember things about them, and ask questions about them as if their life is really important to you. Method 7: Be the transparent friend. One big roadblock for getting to know someone well is when no one is sharing or saying anything out of their comfort zone. if you're just having safe, polite conversations with someone where you both hide your deeper thoughts and emotions, you probably won't build up much trust with them. So try to make your conversations more transparent. And this doesn't mean go up to people and ask them their deepest, darkest regret from their childhood. instead, A great place to start is, Hey, do you want to hang out sometime? Because that's being transparent. That reveals that at least you kind of think the other person is cool. And saying that kind of thing will slowly bring down the brick wall between you and someone you don't know that well. Now on to Method 8: the injury update. people love when you can empathize with their pain and actually seem to care. But usually when you ask, How are you? Everyone just says by default. Good. Or if they're feeling daring. Fine. So instead for this technique, directly ask people if there's anything that's been bothering them recently. Maybe they have a physical injury that they need to complain to someone about. Maybe someone made them mad and they need to vent. Or maybe they've been having a bad day and they just need someone to talk to. don't need to be a therapist, but you just need to care. Number 9: The Group Chat. If you meet new people really often but can't convert them into your friends, this is for you. Obviously what you need to do is hang out with them regularly. But for that, you need some kind of way to contact them. So if you ever get a chance to do some activity with at least three or four people and you feel like everyone had a good time, immediately create a group chat, make sure that you are the first one to suggest the idea And if you need a reason, just see it so that you can get together and do the same thing tomorrow or next week. That way you get everyone's number. And even if the group chat goes quiet, you still have a bunch of people you could potentially ask to hang out with whenever you want. Method 10: do them a favor. It's often said that friendship is about two people who provide value to each other. So when you meet new people, you could use the conversation to figure out how you could be valuable to them. Maybe you're an expert at something they need help with. Maybe you might have a common interest Or maybe you're just nice to them. And if they return the favor or they act really nice towards you, that's probably a good potential friend. method 11: Ask them for a favor. Here's some examples of things you could ask. Could you tell me where this place is? Could you watch my stuff for a minute? Or even could you introduce me to this person? because not only does this show them you trust them, but this allows them to look really nice and generous without much effort. And also people like to feel useful, But whatever you do, don't constantly expect favors from people. If you never do anything nice for anyone, because that's not friendship. That's just networking. Method 12: Pickup Line. this is a method I personally use, If I'm in a group of strangers and I need to start a conversation fast to avoid being alone, I just think of a question that everyone could probably answer and then just go up to different people and ask that question. It's actually pretty easy because you already know what to say, Every time you go up to someone, and everyone will have a different answer that you can naturally lead into a conversation. Then, of course, that opens up space for you to ask better questions about where they're from, who they know, and what they like to do. all because you had the courage to just ask that first random question. Method 13: The Iso-Play. If you're often in a big group and you get overwhelmed feeling like you have to talk to everyone, An Iso-Play in basketball is a common play where you pick out one of the easiest defenders from the other team and try to take them on one on one And to do this in real life, find someone in the group who isn't talking that much and start a side conversation with them only if you have to take them somewhere so that you can talk one on one. Make up some excuse. Like asking them to help you with something. This will make them feel special and important. And if you do this over time with everyone in the group, eventually you'll kind of know everyone decently. Method 14: Act like you're already friends. There's a saying that goes. If you go looking to find friends, you'll find very few. But if you go looking to be a friend, you'll find many. so, instead of trying to find people who will treat you like a friend, you go out instead and treat people like a friend as soon as you meet them. You can do this by flying past the awkward and polite stage and just going to the stage where you're lighting up every time you see someone. You're making fun of them like a friend would. You're saying their name a lot. You're patting them on the back. You're playing fighting with each other. And in general, you're treating them like someone you've known for a while. this is one of the most powerful methods. But as always, be careful because with great power comes great potential to be weird. Method 15: The Activity Buddy. When you meet someone, ask them something. They've always wanted to do but haven't gotten around to doing. then offer to do that thing with them. This way you're basically asking them to hang out, but it doesn't seem desperate because it's framed as you doing a favor for them. Plus, this is a perfectly normal reason for you to get their number. And it works on everyone. And lastly, method 16, which is the fastest possible method we currently know of. Now, you might not be so willing to share personal info with people you don't know. In which case, I'd be doing you a disservice if I didn't tell you about today's sponsor Incogni.
